steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

This is a command to one person to speak. The middle weak letter و is dropped, and the vowels change to make it a command form. This word starts Surah Al-Ikhlas, commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to declare Allah's oneness.
This is a personal pronoun that doesn't come from a root
Added marks above and below to show it means 'He' - pointing to Allah in this ayah. Links 'Say' with 'Allah is One'.
Added shadda to emphasize that this is the one and only deity worthy of worship
Changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word, and added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence. Allah is described as being One (أَحَدٌ) in this ayah.
Connected the letters together and added fatha marks to show how to pronounce it
Added tanween damma to show this is describing Allah as being One. This word comes after هُوَ ٱللَّهُ (He is Allah) in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

This is the unique name of God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it specific to The One True God rather than just any deity. The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject of the sentence.

steps.m: to be sought after for needs, to be eternal and absolute
he was sought after, he was eternal
steps.n: Adding the short vowels 'fatha' (َ) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he was sought after'
Adding the U vowel mark at the end shows this is in the nominative case
Adding ال makes it definite - 'The' instead of 'a'
This is a negative word that changes the verb after it (يَلِدْ) from 'beget' to 'did not beget'. It's like adding 'not' to the past in English.

steps.m: to give birth or produce offspring

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by: 1) adding يَ at start, 2) dropping و because it's weak, 3) changing middle vowel to ِ (kasra), 4) adding ُ (damma) at end

steps.m: he gives birth/produces offspring (negated by لَمْ)
steps.n: Changed the ending to sukun (ْ) because لَمْ before the word makes it negative and changes the ending
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَلِدْ) from present to past tense and makes it negative. It's like adding 'did not' before a verb in English.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects the two statements 'did not beget' and 'was not begotten'.

steps.m: to give birth or be born

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense form
Changed from active past tense 'gave birth' to passive present tense 'is born' by adding يُ at start and changing vowels - shows something happening to someone rather than someone doing it
Added sukun at the end because it comes after لَمْ (not). لَمْ always makes verbs end with sukun.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. It's connected to the verb يَكُن (to be) in this ayah, making it mean 'was not'
The و connects this phrase to what came before it in the surah, adding the meaning of 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels. The و stays because it's a strong position.

steps.m: he is (after لَمْ which makes it past negative)
steps.n: After لَمْ (meaning 'did not'), the word is shortened by dropping the final و and ُ. This changes it from 'he is' to 'he was not'.
This is a preposition showing direction or possession, connected to the verb يَكُن (was) in this ayah
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda
Added هُ with elongation mark ۥ to show this refers to Him (Allah)

steps.m: to match or be equal to
steps.n: This root is about being equal or matching something else
Adding ُ (damma) to first letter starts turning it into a noun
Adding another ُ (damma) continues the noun pattern
Adding و creates the final noun pattern meaning 'equal'
Adding ًا at the end makes it indefinite and accusative case
This root is about being one or alone
Root letters joined together with short 'a' sounds to make the word for 'one'
Added damma tanween to show this is the subject. In this ayah, it's saying 'there is not anyone comparable to Him'
Added a small mark after the last letter to show it blends into the next word when read
